project_summary:
MVP redesign of Aitri Hub: collapse the 7-tab web UI into a single-view home, move project card indicators into a clear priority hierarchy, add a web-based admin panel to replace the CLI setup workflow, and remove dead surfaces (light mode, CLI monitor, CLI setup).

Product Analysis:
  North Star KPI: Zero-terminal configuration — a non-CLI user can register a project and see it on the dashboard entirely through the browser, with no terminal interaction.
  JTBD: 'When I need to see the health of all my Aitri projects, show me what matters immediately and let me configure everything from the browser — no terminal required.'
  Guardrail metric: Dashboard render time must not exceed 2s for up to 20 projects (inherited from FR-006 — must not regress).
no_go_zone: No authentication or sessions — the admin panel is localhost-only; no login, no tokens, no user accounts
No removal of the collector/monitor background process — it continues to run and write dashboard.json; only the terminal-rendered CLI dashboard surface is deprecated
No aitri command execution from the admin panel — the panel manages projects.json only; it cannot trigger aitri run-phase, approve, verify, etc.
No mobile layout — minimum supported viewport is 768px; sub-768px behavior is undefined in this feature
No Graph, Coverage, Velocity, or Activity tab restoration — these views are removed; their data may appear on cards but they are not re-introduced as standalone views
No Docker or nginx changes — the admin API is served by the same Node.js process as the existing web command; nginx config is unchanged

user_personas.goal: Open the browser dashboard in the morning and immediately see which projects need attention, without running any terminal commands
user_personas.pain_point: Current 7-tab layout requires clicking through multiple tabs to get a full picture; must use terminal to add new projects

user_personas.goal: Register all team projects from a web form and share the dashboard URL with teammates who have no CLI experience
user_personas.pain_point: CLI setup is a blocker for non-CLI teammates; no way to onboard them to Hub without giving terminal instructions

user_personas.goal: See a single-screen view of all projects with the most critical health indicators visible without any interaction
user_personas.pain_point: Current card layout shows too many equal-weight metrics; critical alerts do not stand out from informational data

functional_requirements.title: Single-view web home — remove tab navigation
functional_requirements.description: The web dashboard renders one view: the project card grid with header and stat summary. No tab navigation bar is present. The header retains the title, status counts (healthy/warning/error), clock, and refresh button. The stat tiles row (projects, healthy, warning, blocking, pipeline%) and the blocking triage section (shown only when blocking alerts exist) are retained above the card grid. Folder grouping is retained. The phase distribution panel and health score list panel are removed from the overview.
acceptance_criteria: Given the app loads at localhost:3000, then no tab navigation element (role=tablist or .tabs) is present in the DOM
Given 5 projects are registered, then all 5 project cards are visible on the home view without any tab interaction
Given the app loads with 0 projects, then the empty state message is visible without navigating to any tab
Given viewport width is 768px, then the project grid and header render without horizontal scroll
Given blocking alerts exist, then the triage section appears above the card grid
Given no blocking alerts exist, then the triage section is absent from the DOM

functional_requirements.title: Project card — always-visible primary indicators
functional_requirements.description: Each project card renders two always-visible sections. Section 1 (identity + pipeline): project name, status badge (HEALTHY/WARNING/ERROR/UNREADABLE), pipeline progress as 'X/5 phases approved' with a visual progress bar, and health score grade (A/B/C/D/F computed from pipeline progress, test pass rate, blocking alerts, and compliance). Section 2 (key metrics): test summary ('passed/total (%)' with a progress bar, or 'N/A' if absent), blocking alert count as a red badge (rendered only when count > 0), and last commit age (with a stale indicator when age > 72h).
acceptance_criteria: Given a project with approvedPhases length 3, then the card shows '3/5' and a progress bar at 60% fill
Given a project with 2 blocking alerts, then a red badge displaying '2' is visible in the key metrics section
Given a project with 0 blocking alerts, then no blocking alert badge element is rendered in the DOM
Given a project with testSummary.passed=27 and testSummary.total=27, then the tests row shows '27/27 (100%)'
Given a project with no testSummary, then the tests row shows 'N/A'
Given a project whose last commit was 80 hours ago, then the commit age row shows a value ≥72h and includes a visible stale indicator
Given a project with health score ≥90, then the grade badge displays 'A' in green (var(--syn-green))
Given a project with health score <35, then the grade badge displays 'F' in red (var(--syn-red))

functional_requirements.title: Project card — expandable detail section
functional_requirements.description: Each project card has an expandable section, collapsed by default, triggered by a single click on an expand control. When expanded, it reveals: active branch, active feature sub-pipeline count (if any), last pipeline event with phase number and age, compliance badge (if available), FR requirements summary (total FRs, MUST count), commit velocity (commits/7d), open bug count (if any), and time in current phase.
acceptance_criteria: Given a project card in its default state, then the expandable section content is not visible (display:none or equivalent)
Given the user clicks the expand control, then the detail section becomes visible within ≤200ms
Given the user clicks the expand control again, then the section collapses within ≤200ms
Given a project with no active features, then the features row is absent from the expanded section
Given a project with no compliance data, then the compliance row is absent from the expanded section
Given a project with 0 open bugs, then the bugs row is absent from the expanded section

functional_requirements.title: Web admin panel — project listing
functional_requirements.description: The web app serves a route at /admin that renders the admin panel. The panel lists all projects currently registered in ~/.aitri-hub/projects.json, showing each project's name, type (local/remote), and path or URL.
acceptance_criteria: Given the user navigates to http://localhost:3000/admin, then the page renders within ≤2s
Given 3 projects are registered in projects.json, then the admin panel lists all 3 with name, type, and path/URL
Given 0 projects are registered, then the admin panel shows an empty state with a visible call-to-action to add the first project
Given the user navigates from /admin to /, then the project card grid renders without a full page reload (client-side routing)

functional_requirements.title: Web admin panel — add project
functional_requirements.description: From the admin panel, the user can add a new project by providing a display name, type (local or remote), and either a local filesystem path or a GitHub/GitLab URL. On submit, the entry is appended to ~/.aitri-hub/projects.json via the admin API.
acceptance_criteria: Given the user submits a name, type=local, and a path that exists on the filesystem, then a new entry is written to projects.json and the list updates to include it
Given the user submits a local path that does not exist on the filesystem, then an inline error message is displayed and no entry is written to projects.json
Given the user submits a name already present in projects.json, then an inline error message is displayed and no duplicate entry is written
Given the user submits a type=remote URL, then the entry is written to projects.json without filesystem validation of the URL
Given projects.json does not exist, then a successful add creates the file with the new entry as a valid JSON array

functional_requirements.title: Web admin panel — edit and remove project
functional_requirements.description: From the admin panel, the user can edit an existing project's display name and path/URL, or remove it. Removal requires an explicit confirmation step before the entry is deleted from projects.json.
acceptance_criteria: Given the user edits a project name and submits, then projects.json is updated and the list reflects the new name within ≤1s without a full page reload
Given the user clicks the remove action, then a confirmation dialog is shown before any deletion occurs
Given the user confirms removal, then the entry is deleted from projects.json and no longer appears in the admin list
Given the user dismisses the confirmation dialog, then projects.json is unchanged and the entry remains in the list
Given the user submits an edit with an empty name, then an inline error is shown and no write occurs

functional_requirements.title: Admin API — local HTTP endpoint for projects.json CRUD
functional_requirements.description: The aitri-hub web process serves a local HTTP API at /api/projects (and /api/projects/:id) that the admin panel uses to read and write ~/.aitri-hub/projects.json. The server binds exclusively to 127.0.0.1.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/projects returns the current projects.json array as JSON with status 200; returns [] if file does not exist
POST /api/projects with a valid project payload appends the entry to projects.json and returns 201 with the created entry
PUT /api/projects/:id with a valid payload updates the matching entry in projects.json and returns 200
DELETE /api/projects/:id removes the matching entry from projects.json and returns 204
Given a local path payload containing '..' segments, the API returns 400 and performs no filesystem operation
Given a request from an origin other than localhost (127.0.0.1 or ::1), the server does not respond (bind restriction, not HTTP 403)

functional_requirements.title: CLI setup deprecation notice
functional_requirements.description: Running `aitri-hub setup` prints a single deprecation notice directing the user to the web admin panel at http://localhost:3000/admin and exits immediately. No interactive prompts are launched. projects.json is not modified.
acceptance_criteria: Given the user runs `aitri-hub setup`, then stdout contains the string 'http://localhost:3000/admin' and the process exits with code 0
Given the user runs `aitri-hub setup`, then no interactive readline prompt is started and no stdin is consumed
Given projects.json contains 2 entries before the command runs, then it still contains the same 2 entries after the command exits

functional_requirements.title: CLI monitor deprecation notice
functional_requirements.description: Running `aitri-hub monitor` prints a single deprecation notice directing the user to the web dashboard at http://localhost:3000 and exits immediately. No terminal dashboard is rendered and no polling loop is started.
acceptance_criteria: Given the user runs `aitri-hub monitor`, then stdout contains the string 'http://localhost:3000' and the process exits with code 0
Given the user runs `aitri-hub monitor`, then no ANSI screen-clear sequence is written to stdout
Given the user runs `aitri-hub monitor`, then the process terminates within ≤500ms without requiring Ctrl+C

functional_requirements.title: Dark-mode only — remove light mode
functional_requirements.description: The web dashboard renders exclusively in dark mode. The theme toggle button is removed from the header. The [data-theme='light'] CSS block is removed from the stylesheet. No theme preference is read from or written to localStorage.
acceptance_criteria: Given the app loads, then no element with role=button or class containing 'theme' or 'toggle' related to color mode is present in the header DOM
Given the app CSS is parsed, then no selector matching [data-theme='light'] exists in any loaded stylesheet — confirmed by searching the compiled CSS bundle for the string 'data-theme="light"'
Given the app loads in a browser with no prior localStorage state, then localStorage.getItem('aitri-theme') returns null after initial render
Given the app renders, then background-color of the body element resolves to #0D1117 (--bg dark token) at all viewport widths ≥768px

user_stories.i_want: to see all project cards on a single page without navigating tabs
user_stories.so_that: I can assess the health of all projects in one glance without clicking through multiple views

acceptance_criteria.given: the app is loaded at localhost:3000 with 8 registered projects
acceptance_criteria.then: all 8 project cards are visible in the grid and no tab navigation bar is present in the DOM

user_stories.i_want: to see the most critical project indicators at the top of each card without expanding anything
user_stories.so_that: I can immediately spot which projects have blocking issues or failing tests

acceptance_criteria.given: a project card for 'my-api' with 2 blocking alerts and testSummary 15/20 passing
acceptance_criteria.then: a red badge showing '2' and a tests row showing '15/20 (75%)' are visible without any user interaction

acceptance_criteria.given: a project card for 'clean-project' with 0 blocking alerts
acceptance_criteria.then: no blocking alert badge element is present in that card's DOM

user_stories.i_want: to expand a card to see full detail when I need to investigate a specific project
user_stories.so_that: the home stays uncluttered by default but I can access all data without navigating away

acceptance_criteria.given: a project card in its default (collapsed) state
acceptance_criteria.when: the user clicks the expand control
acceptance_criteria.then: the detail section becomes visible within 200ms and shows at minimum the active branch and last pipeline event

user_stories.i_want: to open a web page to see and manage all registered projects
user_stories.so_that: I can onboard teammates without giving them terminal instructions

acceptance_criteria.given: the user navigates to http://localhost:3000/admin with 3 projects in projects.json
acceptance_criteria.then: 3 project entries are listed, each showing name, type, and path/URL, within ≤2s

user_stories.i_want: to add a new project from a web form without touching the terminal
user_stories.so_that: I can register projects for teammates who give me their repo paths

acceptance_criteria.given: the admin panel is open and projects.json has 2 entries
acceptance_criteria.when: the user submits a form with name='billing-api', type=local, path='/Users/jane/billing-api' (which exists)
acceptance_criteria.then: projects.json gains a third entry and the admin list immediately shows 'billing-api'

acceptance_criteria.when: the user submits a form with path='/nonexistent/path'
acceptance_criteria.then: an inline error message is shown, projects.json is not modified, and the form remains open

user_stories.i_want: to remove a stale project from the registry via the browser
user_stories.so_that: the dashboard stays accurate without needing to edit projects.json manually

acceptance_criteria.given: the admin panel lists a project named 'old-project'
acceptance_criteria.when: the user clicks remove and then confirms in the dialog
acceptance_criteria.then: 'old-project' is removed from projects.json and disappears from the admin list

acceptance_criteria.given: the user clicks remove on a project
acceptance_criteria.when: the user dismisses the confirmation dialog
acceptance_criteria.then: the project remains in the list and projects.json is unchanged

user_stories.i_want: a local API to persist project changes to projects.json
user_stories.so_that: all CRUD operations from the browser are durably saved across sessions

acceptance_criteria.given: the admin API receives POST /api/projects with a valid local path
acceptance_criteria.then: projects.json contains the new entry and the response status is 201

acceptance_criteria.given: the admin API receives POST /api/projects with path='../../etc/passwd'
acceptance_criteria.then: the response status is 400 and projects.json is not modified

user_stories.as_a: developer who ran aitri-hub setup out of habit
user_stories.i_want: to see a clear deprecation message pointing me to the web panel
user_stories.so_that: I know where to go without searching documentation

acceptance_criteria.given: the user runs `aitri-hub setup` in a terminal
acceptance_criteria.then: stdout contains 'http://localhost:3000/admin', the process exits with code 0, and no prompt is shown

user_stories.as_a: developer who ran aitri-hub monitor out of habit
user_stories.i_want: to see a clear deprecation message pointing me to the web dashboard
user_stories.so_that: I know the terminal dashboard is gone and where to find its replacement

acceptance_criteria.given: the user runs `aitri-hub monitor` in a terminal
acceptance_criteria.then: stdout contains 'http://localhost:3000', the process exits within 500ms with code 0, and no screen-clear sequence is written

user_stories.i_want: a consistent dark UI with no accidental light flash or theme toggle to click
user_stories.so_that: the dashboard looks intentional and professional from first load

acceptance_criteria.given: the app loads in a browser with no prior localStorage state
acceptance_criteria.then: the background color is var(--bg) (#0D1117) and no theme toggle button is present in the header

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The admin API rejects any project path payload containing '..' path traversal segments before any filesystem operation is performed.
acceptance_criteria: Given POST /api/projects with path='../../../etc/passwd', the response is 400 and no file is read or written
Given POST /api/projects with path='/valid/absolute/path' containing no '..' segments, the request proceeds normally

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Every HTTP request to the admin API is logged to stdout in the format: [ISO8601_TIMESTAMP] METHOD /path STATUS_CODE
acceptance_criteria: Given a GET /api/projects request completes, then stdout contains a line matching the pattern [timestamp] GET /api/projects 200
Given a failed POST /api/projects request (400), then stdout contains a line matching the pattern [timestamp] POST /api/projects 400

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The admin panel is fully usable at 768px viewport width without horizontal scroll or overlapping elements.
acceptance_criteria: Given viewport width set to 768px, then the admin project list, add form, and edit/remove controls are all visible and interactive with no horizontal scrollbar
Given viewport width set to 1440px, then the admin panel layout does not leave more than 60% of the viewport empty (content is not pinned to a narrow column)

constraints: Admin API must be served by the existing aitri-hub web Node.js process — no separate server binary
Admin API binds to 127.0.0.1 only — no 0.0.0.0 bind
projects.json writes must be atomic (temp file + rename) to avoid corruption on crash
No new npm runtime dependencies for the CLI layer — admin API must use Node.js built-in http module
technology_preferences: React Router (already in project) for /admin client-side route
Node.js built-in http module for admin API server (no Express — zero new runtime deps)
Existing CSS design tokens (--syn-*, --bg, --surface, etc.) for admin panel styling — no new CSS framework
